name: endurance
description:
Alfred Lansing's Endurance — an executable toolkit for leadership under extreme adversity, drawn from the harrowing true story of Ernest Shackleton's 1914 Antarctic expedition and the survival of his entire crew against impossible odds.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Leadership in Crisis — how Shackleton kept his crew alive, united, and hopeful through nearly two years of hardship
② Building Unbreakable Team Spirit — create a culture of mutual support and shared purpose
③ Decision-Making Under Uncertainty — make critical decisions with incomplete information
④ Maintaining Hope in Desperate Circumstances — sustain optimism when the situation seems hopeless
⑤ Adaptability & Resourcefulness — pivot strategies when plans fail.

Trigger when users say: "Shackleton" "Endurance expedition" "Leadership under pressure" "Crisis leadership" "Team survival" "Never give up" "Extreme adversity" "Leadership lessons" "Survival mindset"
or mention: Ernest Shackleton / Endurance / Antarctica / survival / crisis leadership / polar exploration.

Related skills: cant-hurt-me, leadership-in-turbulent-times, the-slight-edge, winning, the-servant.
useCase: Helps leaders navigate extreme adversity by applying Ernest Shackleton's proven methods for keeping teams alive, united, and hopeful when everything goes wrong.
knownRisks: Shackleton's methods were extreme for extreme circumstances; adapt the principles to your context.
